content:
This appears to be a local error message, not a server error, but the cause is mysterious. I find several references to it on the Internet, but more than one solution. Some things you can try:

- If Thunderbird had crashed, there might be an invisible instance of it running in the background. The easy solution is to simply reboot the computer. Have you done that?

- Another possibility is that your anti-virus has found something it doesn't like among your emails and has taken exclusive control over your mail file. This can be tested by temporarily disabling your AV, or (better) restarting the computer in Safe Mode with Networking.

- I find reports of this happening when there is a malformed email on the server that, I guess, Thunderbird chokes on and stops downloading. Solutions all have to do with going to your email provider's web mail interface. You might see the problem right away - a couple of reports say that there are many copies of one particular email. Deleting that corrupt message 'unstops the dam' and normal operation resumes. Here's a conversation about that:

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/questions/1463022

Other reports say they fixed it by moving all emails in the inbox (in web mail) to a temporary folder, then telling Thunderbird to fetch mail, then moving the mails back to the Inbox in small batches until the errant message is identified and deleted. Here's a conversation about that:

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/questions/1521373

Other people said the problem just went away on its own!
creator: rednoise

content: Just a possibility to test: If email sent to account A also appears in account B, then uninstall account B and see if account A still functions. My thought is that one of the two accounts may be an alias for the other.

content: Just a possibility here: sometimes, on upgrade, Thunderbird starts with a fresh profile. Bypass that option and click help>troubleshootinformation, scroll down to 'profiles' and click 'about:profiles. On that screen, you should see a list of profiles, and one of them is probably your old one. You can click to open the profile and browe the imapmail folder for presence of any IMAP accounts or he Mail folder for any POP accounts —OR you can just click to launch the profile and see what happens.
creator: davidsk

content:
Hi Alexander,

Publishing your Apple ID and a password is not a good idea. It does not matter much in this case because that app-specific password was revoked when you changed your Apple ID password. You will either have to generate a new app-specific password or see if this other method works (I don't know if it does): https://support.apple.com/en-us/121539

content:
Thunderbird for Windows, macOS, Linux has always been free from the start.

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/thunderbird-faq https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/thunderbird-faq#w_is-thunderbird-free

Is Thunderbird free? Yes! Thunderbird is open source software. Anyone can download and use the program for free, and view and modify the source code under the terms of the license.

As said the donation is optional and is not a requirement in order to download and use Thunderbird.

If you change your mind on the donation then see the FAQ on https://www.thunderbird.net/donate/

Can I request a refund? Your donation may be eligible for a refund. You must contact us within 15 days of payment date. If you believe this payment was made without your authorization, please contact us immediately. Donations made via ACH or SEPA direct debit are ineligible for refunds. Due to the nature of these transactions our policy prioritizes minimizing refund fraud. We appreciate your understanding. If you need assistance, please contact us. https://www.thunderbird.net/donate/help

content: The fake account sounds like it is an alias account for your primary account. That allows you to use it, but it is dependent on the primary account. My suggestion is to set it up in Thunderbird by clicking 'account settings', then highlight your primary account in left side, and then click the 'manage identities' button on right side of screen. There, set up your alias. This lets you compose messages, using the dropdown menu on FROM line and incoming messages will appear within the primary account.
creator: davidsk
